purpose: W605 implementation-grade operator kernel playbook. This converts the research frontier into concrete algorithm kernels that can be implemented by backend agents across quantization, compilation, distillation, serving, evaluation, security, retrieval, edge deployment, and cloud compute.

sources.lesson: KVQuant shows that KV-cache quantization needs key-specific layouts, pre-RoPE key treatment, non-uniform datatypes, sparse outlier handling, and centroid normalization; Kolm should treat KV compression as an artifact/runtime policy, not a generic int4 flag.

KVLinC combines Hadamard rotation and linear correction adapters to offset extreme low-precision KV cache error; Kolm's quantization planner should support corrective adapters and kernel-specific acceptance gates.

Adaptive KV precision improves the accuracy-latency tradeoff versus static KV quantization for lightweight on-device models; Kolm should choose precision per layer/head/token regime instead of shipping one fixed cache bitwidth.

GPU-accelerated INT8 KV compression demonstrates that simple cache compression can be low overhead when kernels are vectorized and tiled; Kolm should include a pragmatic fast path before exotic ultra-low-bit paths.
MLIR dialect conversion provides legal target sets, rewrite patterns, and partial/full conversion modes; KolmIR should express target legality and blocked lowering explicitly.
TVM Relax separates graph-level transformation from low-level tensor scheduling; Kolm's compiler needs a behavior graph layer before runtime-specific lowering.
IREE is a retargetable MLIR compiler/runtime across datacenter, mobile, and edge; Kolm should target compiler backends through a capability manifest rather than hardcoded device branches.

TensorRT-LLM exposes production-serving primitives including paged attention, quantization, guided decoding, speculative decoding, KV cache systems, LoRA, parallelism, and benchmarking; Kolm should model those as separate runtime capabilities.

vLLM made memory-efficient serving and OpenAI-compatible high-throughput inference a standard expectation; Kolm should integrate with vLLM-style serving where compiled artifacts target server GPU lanes.
SGLang-style runtimes combine structured outputs, batching, radix/prefix cache reuse, speculative decoding, and OpenAI-compatible APIs; Kolm should generate runtime policy from artifact metadata.

Experiential KD frames distillation as learning in the teacher's original environment using reward imitation; Kolm distillation should preserve environment, rubric, and reward provenance, not only teacher completions.
Generalized Knowledge Distillation addresses distribution mismatch by training on student-generated outputs with teacher feedback; Kolm should include on-policy replay loops before promotion.
Reusable eval definitions and benchmark registries are required for credible comparison; Kolm should emit eval packs that are reproducible outside Kolm.
Inspect's composable tasks, scorers, model adapters, and logs are a neutral safety/capability eval substrate; Kolm proof packs should round-trip to neutral runners.
Conformal intervals for LLM-as-judge make uncertainty explicit; Kolm K-score should record intervals, abstention thresholds, and reprompt policy rather than only scalar judge scores.

OWASP LLM risks define the minimum security taxonomy for prompt injection, data leakage, poisoning, supply chain, excessive agency, and unbounded consumption; Kolm should map every artifact and agent tool to those controls.
garak provides an actively maintained vulnerability-scanning baseline for LLM systems; Kolm should generate scanner manifests and attach red-team receipts before promotion.
PyRIT is a risk-identification framework for generative AI; Kolm should support multi-turn attack orchestration and store attack traces as first-class eval evidence.
DSPy treats prompting as an optimizable program; Kolm should compile prompts, retrieval choices, examples, and eval gates into a search space before deciding to train or distill.
KServe gives Kubernetes-native model serving and autoscaling contracts; Kolm cloud deploy should generate KServe-compatible manifests when enterprise clusters are the target.
Ray Serve LLM covers distributed OpenAI-compatible serving, observability, and fault tolerance; Kolm should represent Ray as a first-class cloud/runtime lane for large deployments.
MLC LLM combines ML compilation with native deployment APIs; Kolm edge packaging should choose MLC-style native deploys where portability and app embedding matter.
ONNX Runtime GenAI owns the generation loop, logits processing, KV cache, sampling, chat templates, and structured outputs; Kolm must model runtime-loop features, not only file extensions.

operator_kernels.title: HoloQuant Joint Weight, Activation, And KV Planner
operator_kernels.thesis: A single quantization decision is weaker than a joint memory plan. This kernel ranks weight quantization, activation treatment, KV-cache bitwidth, corrective adapters, and runtime kernel availability together so users get a proven memory/quality/latency frontier for each artifact.
data_contracts: QuantPlanInput: model_id, params_b, context_tokens, target_device, runtime, calibration_rows, privacy_mode
QuantPlanOutput: ranked_methods, kv_policy, estimated_memory_gb, expected_quality, proof_requirements
build_steps: Add a scoring function that penalizes memory overflow before latency.
Model KV cache memory independently from weight memory.
Represent corrective adapters as optional post-quant artifacts.
Attach runtime kernel availability from platform capabilities.
Emit a holdout eval plan and block promotion without it.
Record source model hash, calibration hash, and quant policy in receipts.
smoke_tests: 7B extraction on RTX 4090 with 8192 context chooses AWQ or equivalent and fits under 24GB.
Tiny on-device SmolLM profile chooses adaptive KV policy and refuses unsupported kernels.
failure_modes: planner recommends a kernel not available on the selected runtime
quality estimate is emitted without a holdout gate
KV memory is double-counted or omitted
secret model-provider values leak into proof output

operator_kernels.title: KolmIR Legalization And Autotarget Kernel
operator_kernels.thesis: Kolm should compile behavior into an intermediate representation that knows target legality, blocked lowering, runtime-loop features, and proof requirements before choosing ONNX, IREE, MLC, TensorRT-LLM, WASM, or local JS packaging.
data_contracts: TargetPlanInput: artifact_manifest, model_family, runtime_constraints, hardware_profile, offline_required
TargetPlanOutput: selected_target, legal_ops, blocked_ops, fallback_targets, proof_steps
build_steps: Define behavior-level nodes separate from tensor-level ops.
Load runtime target capabilities from product graph and platform capabilities.
Compute legality and blocked-lowering reasons per target.
Rank targets by feasible runtime features, latency, install friction, and proof strength.
Emit fallback target chain for unsupported operators.
Bind selected target and blocked reasons into the artifact receipt.
smoke_tests: Structured extraction artifact picks ONNX Runtime GenAI on Windows when generation-loop features are available.
Offline browser artifact refuses TensorRT and selects WASM/JS with a clear blocked-op explanation.
failure_modes: auto target silently picks an unavailable runtime
blocked lowering reason is missing
fallback target lacks verifier support
target plan changes without receipt hash drift

operator_kernels.title: Experiential On-Policy Distillation Kernel
operator_kernels.thesis: Kolm distillation should learn from the environment, rubric, and student mistakes, not just teacher completions. The kernel combines GKD-style on-policy replay with X-KD-style reward imitation and provenance-bound teacher feedback.
data_contracts: DistillKernelInput: task, teacher_provider, student_model, real_pairs, holdout_pairs, reward_rubric, privacy_mode
DistillKernelOutput: mode, replay_schedule, teacher_queries, reward_model_spec, promotion_gates
build_steps: Start from capture/holdout disjointness checks.
Generate student outputs for selected prompts before asking teacher feedback.
Store teacher feedback as reward evidence with provider/model/version hashes.
Mix supervised, reverse-KL, and reward-imitation losses by task type.
Use holdout and adversarial evals before each promotion.
Emit cost forecast and stop rules before teacher calls.
smoke_tests: Generation task with 1500 real pairs and 300 holdout pairs selects experiential KD and emits teacher query budget.
Low-data task with 12 pairs refuses distillation and recommends prompt/eval capture first.
failure_modes: teacher feedback rows leak into holdout
student-generated replay is missing
reward rubric is unversioned
promotion proceeds without disjoint holdout

operator_kernels.title: Speculative Structured Serving Kernel
operator_kernels.thesis: The fastest useful runtime combines prefix/KV reuse, speculative decoding, guided decoding, batching, and artifact-specific schema constraints. Kolm should emit a runtime policy that chooses those knobs per artifact instead of exposing them as unrelated options.
data_contracts: ServePlanInput: artifact_manifest, schema_contract, traffic_shape, latency_slo_ms, runtime_targets
ServePlanOutput: runtime, batching_policy, cache_policy, speculative_policy, structured_decode_policy
build_steps: Read schema/eval contracts from artifact manifest.
Score runtime support for guided decoding and speculative decoding.
Estimate prefix cache benefit from traffic shape.
Pick batching and cache policy under latency SLO.
Emit a policy receipt with fallback behavior.
Expose per-call runtime decisions in telemetry.
smoke_tests: JSON extraction artifact selects guided decoding and disables unsafe freeform sampling.
High-repeat support workload enables prefix cache and reports expected cost reduction.
failure_modes: schema enforcement disabled for structured artifact
speculative policy changes output distribution without verifier guard
batching violates latency SLO
cache reuse ignores tenant isolation

operator_kernels.title: Judge Interval Proof Kernel
operator_kernels.thesis: A scalar K-score is not enough for high-stakes claims. Kolm should emit calibrated intervals, abstention zones, deterministic eval scores, judge disagreement, and replayable OpenAI Evals/Inspect packs before a compiled artifact can claim production quality.
data_contracts: EvalProofInput: artifact_hash, eval_cases, deterministic_scores, judge_scores, calibration_cases, risk_policy
EvalProofOutput: score_interval, midpoint, abstentions, export_paths, promotion_verdict
build_steps: Split calibration rows from holdout rows.
Compute deterministic metrics where available.
Run judge prompts with model/version hashes.
Fit conformal intervals and midpoint score.
Mark abstentions when interval crosses risk threshold.
Export OpenAI Evals and Inspect-compatible packs.
smoke_tests: Ambiguous generation eval emits a wide interval and blocks production promotion.
Deterministic extraction eval emits a narrow interval and preserves replay pack hashes.
failure_modes: judge score has no calibration split
interval ignored in production verdict
export pack is not reproducible
judge prompt changes without receipt hash

operator_kernels.title: OWASP Red-Team Compiler Kernel
operator_kernels.thesis: Security should be compiled into artifact promotion. This kernel maps artifact capabilities to OWASP risk categories, generates garak/PyRIT attack plans, stores attack traces, and refuses promotion when agent tools exceed their declared authority.
data_contracts: RedteamPlanInput: artifact_manifest, tool_permissions, data_classes, deployment_mode, risk_profile
RedteamPlanOutput: owasp_controls, garak_manifest, pyrit_strategy, required_fixes, promotion_verdict
build_steps: Parse artifact tools, data classes, and permissions.
Map capabilities to OWASP LLM risk categories.
Generate garak probes for model/system behavior.
Generate PyRIT multi-turn strategies for agent workflows.
Store attack traces and remediation receipts.
Block promotion when high-risk controls lack passing evidence.
smoke_tests: Agent artifact with network tool gets excessive-agency and prompt-injection probes.
PHI redactor artifact requires leakage and insecure-output handling probes before promotion.
failure_modes: risk category omitted for an enabled tool
attack trace is not replayable
promotion ignores failed high-severity probe
red-team output leaks secrets

operator_kernels.title: Capture-To-Curriculum Data Mixture Kernel
operator_kernels.thesis: Kolm's capture lake should become an active curriculum engine. The kernel ranks rows by novelty, privacy risk, label uncertainty, expected eval lift, and duplication so training budgets go to the highest-value rows.
data_contracts: MixturePlanInput: capture_namespace, privacy_policy, eval_targets, label_budget, task_type
MixturePlanOutput: selected_rows, rejected_rows, risk_summary, expected_lift, labeling_plan
build_steps: Cluster near-duplicate captures before sampling.
Score rows for privacy redaction confidence and policy risk.
Estimate label uncertainty using current artifact and judge interval.
Estimate eval lift by mapping row features to failing eval clusters.
Emit active-label batches with provenance.
Exclude holdout-only rows from training plans.
smoke_tests: Namespace with duplicate support tickets selects diverse high-uncertainty rows.
PHI-heavy namespace routes rows through redaction review before label export.
failure_modes: holdout rows selected for training
privacy risk ignored in active learning
duplicate rows dominate label budget
expected lift emitted without eval target

operator_kernels.title: RAG-To-Artifact Retrieval Compiler Kernel
operator_kernels.thesis: RAG is not a separate product from compiling. Kolm should infer whether a task needs no retrieval, lexical retrieval, dense retrieval, ColBERT-style late interaction, GraphRAG, or prompt-only optimization, then bind the chosen retrieval plan into the artifact receipt.
data_contracts: RagPlanInput: task, corpus_profile, latency_slo, privacy_mode, target_runtime
RagPlanOutput: retrieval_mode, index_spec, attribution_policy, eval_cases, compile_or_no_train_verdict
build_steps: Profile corpus size, structure, update rate, and privacy class.
Run no-train decision before suggesting distillation.
Rank retrieval modes by expected quality, latency, and deploy friction.
Generate attribution and citation requirements.
Create eval cases for retrieval misses and hallucination risk.
Bind index hash and retrieval policy into artifact manifest.
smoke_tests: Small static FAQ chooses lexical/local retrieval and no training.
Large cross-linked policy corpus chooses graph/dense hybrid with attribution gates.
failure_modes: training recommended when retrieval solves the task
index hash omitted from receipt
private corpus pushed to public runtime
retrieval mode lacks latency estimate

operator_kernels.title: Device-Native Artifact Packaging Kernel
operator_kernels.thesis: On-device AI is won by native runtime packaging and honest fallback. Kolm should package artifacts for ONNX Runtime GenAI, MLC, ExecuTorch-style targets, browser/WASM, and JS fallback with target-specific generation-loop capability evidence.
data_contracts: EdgePackageInput: artifact_manifest, target_os, accelerator, max_bundle_mb, offline_required
EdgePackageOutput: runtime_package, included_files, omitted_features, fallback_chain, proof_checks
build_steps: Detect target OS, accelerator, memory, and runtime install state.
Select generation-loop backend from runtime feature matrix.
Partition model, tokenizer, receipt, verifier, and fallback assets.
Enforce bundle size and offline constraints.
Generate install and verification instructions.
Run local smoke with fixture inputs where runtime exists.
smoke_tests: Browser target refuses a 7B bundle and emits WASM/remote fallback.
Windows local ONNX target includes tokenizer and structured-output capability proof.
failure_modes: bundle exceeds declared size budget
runtime-loop feature missing but not reported
offline artifact depends on network
receipt verifier omitted from package

operator_kernels.title: Cloud Train And Serve Orchestrator Kernel
operator_kernels.thesis: Users without GPUs need a real train/serve path. Kolm should broker storage, hosted GPU, managed training, self-hosted SSH, KServe, Ray Serve, and provider teacher calls through one policy that prices, gates, and records every external dependency.
data_contracts: CloudOrchestrateInput: workload, params_b, rows, privacy_mode, storage_targets, compute_targets, budget_usd
CloudOrchestrateOutput: selected_lane, rejected_lanes, cost_estimate, setup_requirements, receipt_fields
build_steps: Detect configured storage and compute providers without exposing secrets.
Rank local, SSH, hosted GPU, managed train, KServe, and Ray lanes.
Require storage readiness before remote train jobs.
Attach data residency and privacy constraints.
Emit exact job command and webhook plan.
Record provider, image, region, dataset hash, and artifact hash.
smoke_tests: No-local-GPU train workload with R2 and RunPod selects hosted GPU and emits setup-safe command.
Airgap enterprise workload refuses hosted GPU and recommends SSH or customer cluster.
failure_modes: remote job starts without durable storage
airgap policy is bypassed
secret values appear in plan output
cost estimate lacks provider and region

operator_kernels.title: Artifact Governance And Attestation Kernel
operator_kernels.thesis: The .kolm artifact becomes valuable when governance evidence is automatic. This kernel binds SBOM, SLSA-style build provenance, runtime adoption proof, compliance packet state, red-team evidence, and external gate status into a single promotion ledger.
data_contracts: GovernanceLedgerInput: artifact_hash, evidence_refs, deployment_targets, compliance_profile
GovernanceLedgerOutput: evidence_dag, gate_status, missing_external_evidence, promotion_verdict
build_steps: Normalize local proof packets into an evidence DAG.
Keep external gates distinct from local implementation state.
Bind red-team, eval, package, runtime, and compliance evidence.
Hash every evidence node and parent relation.
Expose buyer-readable missing evidence.
Block promotion when required gates are warn or missing.
smoke_tests: Artifact with local eval but no public benchmark reports local-ready and external-not-ready.
Enterprise artifact with missing certification blocks final compliance claim.
failure_modes: external gate represented as shipped without evidence
evidence DAG has dangling references
promotion ignores compliance profile
ledger contains mutable timestamp-only proof

operator_kernels.title: Agent Implementation Router Kernel
operator_kernels.thesis: Kolm should turn research contracts into implementation work automatically. This kernel mines codegraph ownership, maps each invention to owner files and tests, creates minimal write scopes, and emits agent-ready tasks that do not collide.
data_contracts: AgentPlanInput: contract_id, repo_graph, changed_files, desired_parallelism, risk_tolerance
AgentPlanOutput: work_items, file_ownership, tests_to_run, conflict_risks, done_when
build_steps: Load codegraph and current dirty file list.
Resolve contract owner files and proposed files.
Partition write scopes by module boundary.
Map tests and product probes to each work item.
Score conflict risk against current dirty files.
Emit implementation prompts and done-when gates.
smoke_tests: Cloud orchestrator contract becomes two non-overlapping work items: broker policy and route/CLI parity.
Dirty frontend files are ignored for backend-only contract planning.
failure_modes: two work items own the same file without coordination
task lacks tests
agent prompt omits external gate honesty
dirty unrelated files are marked for revert
